title: Deep Learning Detection of Uncataloged Astronomical Objects in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey

abstract.text: We present a novel machine learning framework for detecting previously uncataloged astronomical objects in large-scale sky surveys. Using a multi-task convolutional neural network with attention mechanisms, we analyze Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) DR19 imaging to identify candidate planets, unusual galaxies, and anomalous stellar objects. Our approach combines deep feature extraction with variational autoencoder-based novelty detection to flag objects inconsistent with known populations. We validate the algorithm through cross-matching with SIMBAD, NED, and Exoplanet Archive, presenting N candidate detections with coordinates, imaging, and confidence metrics. This work demonstrates the potential for AI-assisted discovery in archival astronomical data.

key_points: Problem: Vast archives of astronomical imaging contain undiscovered objects
Challenge: Human inspection doesn't scale to billions of sources
Solution: ML system trained to detect anomalies and novel objects
Validation: Independent catalog cross-matching for verification

subsections.data: SDSS DR19 imaging, 3M+ galaxies, selection criteria
subsections.inference: Sliding window detection, NMS, coordinate refinement
subsections.validation: Cross-match with SIMBAD, NED, Exoplanet Archive, VizieR

key_points: Future work: Spectroscopic follow-up, time-domain analysis
